methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo, having raised $26M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome within the Biotech & Health sector.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $26M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $65M valuation. The Peer-Median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables—while 16 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three matched on stage, proximity, or valuation tier simultaneously. Public-Comp Anchoring, though supported by a 7.4x P/S median across the three public biotech tickers, could not be applied given the absence of disclosed revenue for the private target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, centered on $65M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and limited peer visibility rather than high conviction. Key uncertainties include the true current pre-money valuation (not disclosed), capital efficiency relative to staged peers, and revenue trajectory assumptions embedded in any forward multiple. Additional disclosure of burn rate or development milestones would improve precision.
